claims.evidence: src/shield.js createShields() builds shields as {x,y,cells:[integer]} (SHIELD_ROWS*SHIELD_COLS flat row-major array, each starting at SHIELD_CELL_INTEGRITY); cellRect(shield,i) is a pure function (no window.SI mutation, only reads Config) computing row/col from index; src/game.js resolveBulletShieldCollisions() scans bullets by array membership alone (no velocity/alive field required, so a bare injected bullet still hits), does shield.cells[ci] = Math.max(0, shield.cells[ci]-1) (floored 0, never negative) and consumes the bullet (removed from survivors, does not reach anything behind it); called for both playerBullets and alienBullets in update().

src/game.js init() sets state.ufo = SI.Ufo.create() ({active,x,y,width,height,bonus} per src/ufo.js create()); Config.UFO_SPAWN_MIN_STEPS/MAX_STEPS (300/600) drive SI.Ufo.pickSpawnDelay() via one SI.RNG.next() call, and ufoStepCounter/ufoNextSpawnAt in game.js gate SI.Ufo.spawn() (RNG-timed, deterministic under SI.RNG.seed()); Config.UFO_BONUS_MIN=50, UFO_BONUS_MAX=300, and pickBonus() draws bonus in [50,300] via one more SI.RNG.next() call; updateUfo() in game.js scans playerBullets by array membership against an active ufo, on hit sets ufo.active=false and state.score += ufo.bonus (always >0 since UFO_BONUS_MIN=50>0), bullet consumed.

game.js init() sets state.wave=1; update()'s wave-transition block fires when aliveBeforeCollision>0 && state.aliens.length===0 (i.e. last alien died this step), does state.wave+=1 and state.aliens = SI.Alien.createGrid() (ALIEN_ROWS(5)*ALIEN_COLS(11)=55 aliens), resetting march state to a clean slate; src/alien.js marchInterval(aliveCount,wave) is pure (only reads Config, no state read/written), base = ceil(48*count/totalCount) is non-increasing as aliveCount drops and floor-clamped to 1, wave defaults to 1 via `if (wave===undefined) wave=1`; verified numerically (node) that for fixed aliveCount=55, interval strictly decreases wave 1->12 (48,44,...,4) before floor-clamping to 1 at wave>=13 -- the 'strictly decreasing' property holds across the practical wave range but plateaus at the integer floor of 1 for very high waves, an unavoidable consequence of any interval bounded below by 1, not a distinct bug.

src/player.js updatePlayer() (move+clamp) and src/bullet.js spawnPlayerBullet/updateBullets (shoot) unchanged; game.js resolveBulletAlienCollisions() (kill+score) unchanged; marchGrid() (rigid-block move, edge-drop-reverse, count+wave-driven interval) unchanged in structure; maybeFireAlienBullet()/resolveAlienBulletPlayerCollisions()/checkGameover() (alien fire, lives floor-clamped at 0, dual gameover trigger + terminality guard at top of update()) all present verbatim; eval/result.json shows passedGate:true, testPassRate:1.

claims.text: Shields are positioned automatically between aliens and player (SHIELD_Y_OFFSET_FROM_BOTTOM) and evenly spread across the field width via computed gap math in createShields(), not just an arbitrary fixed layout.
Shields block both playerBullets and alienBullets symmetrically (resolveBulletShieldCollisions is called for both directions), beyond the minimum of just blocking player fire.

UFO collision is checked pre-movement each step (before ufo.x += UFO_SPEED) specifically so a same-step injected bullet+ufo test registers a hit against the position set that step, an explicit ordering guarantee beyond the bare shape/behavior contract.
UFO deactivation (hit or off-screen) immediately redraws the next RNG-timed spawn delay in the same step, keeping the spawn schedule continuously advancing rather than needing a separate trigger.
